title: Personal Money Management
description: Students build practical personal finance skills including budgeting, understanding net pay, saving accounts, compound interest, and the 50/30/20 rule.
rationale: This extra learning module teaches practical money management skills that every NZ student needs regardless of their NCEA pathway. It covers budgeting with real NZ figures, understanding gross versus net pay and tax codes, savings accounts and interest, compound interest with actual NZ bank rates, and the 50/30/20 budgeting rule as a practical framework. No NCEA credits — pure life skills.
learningObjectives: Build a personal budget using the 50/30/20 rule with actual NZD figures
Understand the difference between gross pay and net pay and calculate PAYE tax deductions
Compare NZ savings account types and calculate simple and compound interest
Understand KiwiSaver basics and why starting early matters
Apply practical money management strategies to real NZ student scenarios
assessmentTask: Students build a complete personal financial plan for a fictional NZ school leaver entering the workforce. The plan includes a monthly budget using the 50/30/20 rule, a net pay calculation, a savings goal with compound interest projection, and a written reflection on their own financial goals.

lessons.title: Your Money, Your Life — Why Personal Finance Matters
Building a Budget That Actually Works
Week 1 Challenge — Build Your Personal Financial Plan
Debt and Credit — Staying Out of Trouble
Renting and Flatting — What You Need to Know
Project — Your Complete Personal Financial Plan
Module Review — Money Habits That Last
